report_url.description: Finds the specific landing page URL for a named security report.

report_discovery_pdf.description: Pass 1 — targets direct PDF files. filetype:pdf operator maximises direct-download hits.

report_discovery_landing.description: Pass 2 — targets landing pages and gated download pages. No filetype filter so gated pages surface. Run only when pass 1 finds nothing above threshold.

generic.description: General-purpose search returning top results with no domain scoring.

find_type_classification._comment: Rules used by ResultClassifier to determine whether a URL is a direct PDF, a gated landing page, or unknown. Evaluated in order: pdf_indicators checked first, then landing_page_indicators. All matches are case-insensitive substring checks against the lowercased URL.
find_type_classification.gated_bonus_comment: Score bonus applied when gated_page_signals appear in the snippet, confirming a real gated download page rather than a generic web page.

discovery_heuristics._comment: Scoring config used exclusively by report-discovery.py. PDF and landing page candidates share the same positive/negative keyword signals; find_type-specific bonuses are applied on top.
discovery_heuristics.score_threshold_comment: Raised from 15 to 20 to reduce false positives. Domain match bonus (+25) means on-domain results still clear the bar easily; off-domain raw PDFs now need stronger keyword evidence.
discovery_heuristics.org_domain_match_bonus_comment: Applied when a result URL's registered domain matches the org's known domain from the README org_url column. This strongly favours the vendor's own publication page over third-party mirrors.
discovery_heuristics.org_domain_mismatch_pdf_penalty_comment: Applied to FIND_PDF results whose domain does NOT match the org's known domain. Raw PDFs hosted on unrelated sites are a primary source of false positives (mirrors, aggregators, cached copies of older reports). Landing pages on aggregator sites are NOT penalised because they may legitimately point to the vendor's page.
discovery_heuristics.wrong_year_in_url_penalty_comment: Applied when the URL contains a 4-digit year that differs from the target year. This is the most reliable signal that filetype:pdf surfaced a previous edition rather than the target year.
discovery_heuristics.self_reference_domains_comment: URLs matching any of these substrings are hard-rejected. The Google CSE occasionally surfaces the repo itself as a result because the README lists all the PDFs.
